name: Evidence-Backed Herbal Teas
description: Pharmacologically active herbal infusions with clinical trial evidence: ginger for nausea, valerian for sleep, chamomile for anxiety, and peppermint for IBS — real compounds, not placebo.
replaces: OTC sleep aids, anti-nausea meds, anxiety supplements ($5–15)
shelfLife: Dried herbs: 1–3 years; prepared tea: drink fresh
effectivenessNote: Each herb has specific clinical evidence for its primary indication. Ginger compares favorably to ondansetron for nausea. Valerian shows efficacy in multiple sleep trials. These are pharmacologically active preparations.
warnings: These are pharmacologically active — they contain real compounds that interact with receptors
Valerian may interact with sedative medications
Ginger may thin blood at high doses
Chamomile: avoid if allergic to ragweed/daisies (same family)
Buy from reputable sources to avoid contamination
scienceNote: Ginger: gingerols and shogaols act on 5-HT3 receptors (same target as ondansetron). Valerian: valerenic acid modulates GABA-A receptors. Chamomile: apigenin binds benzodiazepine sites on GABA receptors. Peppermint: menthol relaxes smooth muscle via calcium channel blockade.
